items.ref: Google Doc: Spec: Campaign Management Utility (drive 1cNpjEn_Smy6mHx5rWzflp20hbx65dlbG)
items.summary: Requirements: campaign ops are fragmented across CommCare, KYC systems, payment platforms, reporting/GIS tools and spreadsheets; the tool centralizes them into one unified interface for real-time visibility + admin workflows.
items.claim_hint: The problem is fragmentation across disconnected systems; the tool is the single pane of glass that unifies them.

items.summary: Worker registration stays in CommCare; KYC validation stays in an external system; the tool is an orchestration/visibility layer that consumes data via APIs and triggers writes through existing system APIs (does not replace payment processing or KYC).
items.claim_hint: The tool reads from systems of record and orchestrates — it never becomes a competing source of truth.

items.ref: Campaign Utility Tool_Data Model.pdf (12 datasets / 6 domains / 2 owners)
items.summary: Ownership split: CommCare HQ owns Campaign/Region/Donor/Worker/KYC/AppUser; the Utility owns Payment/Activity/Microplan/Reporting/AuditLog/Connection. Cross-owner FKs (Payment→Worker, Payment→KYC, Microplan→Region) require a defined API/sync contract.
items.claim_hint: There is a precise, documented data-ownership boundary that the tool must honor.

items.ref: Spec doc §3-5 (tabs, use cases, MVP scope)
items.summary: Tabs: Overview, Workers (Payments/KYC/Profiles), Activity (Details/Microplanning & Budget), Reporting & Monitoring, System Administration. MVP includes payment review+approval, daily approval, fraud flagging on shared identifiers, KYC submission, reporting export.
items.claim_hint: The compelling workflows are payment review-with-fraud-guard, KYC validation, microplanning/budget, and reporting/coverage.

items.summary: Design doc: standalone Django app at /campaign/, CommCare HQ OAuth login + in-app whitelist + server-side RBAC (5 roles), prototype shipped verbatim, own ORM; later sources Worker/KYC/Region/Donor from live CommCare Case/Form API reads.
items.claim_hint: The architecture is deliberately isolated and reads the roster from CommCare via the Case API.

items.summary: Workers are generated as synthetic CommCare cases (case_type campaign_worker) carrying the full Worker + KYC field set, placed on real Nigeria geography (labs AdminBoundary state→LGA→ward + in-ward GPS), deterministic, national-scale.
items.claim_hint: The synthetic data is faithful: workers are real-shaped CommCare cases on real geography, not invented rows.

items.summary: A synthetic CommCare project space served via the Case API: CommCareProvider reads workers as cases through commcare_api, short-circuited in-app from WorkerCase for a synthetic domain (symmetric to labs synthetic Connect opportunities). 'Go real' is a per-domain config flip.
items.claim_hint: The tool reads workers FROM the CommCare Case API exactly as it will read real CommCare — the demo path == the production path.

items.summary: Payment + KYC writes run the fraud-guard mutation logic on the CommCare case (WorkerCaseHandle): approving a payment is blocked when a worker has open fraud flags or rejected KYC; writes land on the case, never a tool-local copy.
items.claim_hint: The fraud guard is real and enforced server-side — flagged/failed-KYC workers cannot be paid.

items.summary: A national campaign builds across all 37 states (worker cases + region plans + microplans + reporting + households). Measured cliff: 50k workers = ~38 MB single-payload bootstrap (queries flat at ~9-11), fixed by a summary bootstrap + paginated /api/workers/.
items.claim_hint: The tool works at true national scale (50k workers) and stays usable in the browser.

items.summary: Overview renders server-computed KPIs over ALL workers (KYC/payment donuts, funding, enrolment/attendance/verification/payment progress, workforce by role) — accurate at any scale without shipping the worker list.
items.claim_hint: One screen shows the whole campaign's real-time state.

items.summary: Reporting: enrolment/attendance/payment trends, household coverage, real CSV export + custom-report builder (5 report types, column/group selection), and a geographic coverage MAP (region choropleth shaded by worker count + worker GPS colored by KYC, reusing the labs Mapbox stack).
items.claim_hint: Operational + donor reporting and geographic coverage are first-class — export a CSV, see coverage on a map.

items.summary: CommCare HQ OAuth login + in-app whitelist + server-side RBAC (5 roles × module × verb) + real audit logging written on every privileged admin action (payments, KYC, user mgmt, activities, microplans) with masked IP.
items.claim_hint: Access is controlled and every privileged action is audited — accountability is built in.

items.summary: The tool is deployed and live; the national campaign is viewable at /campaign/?campaign=MR-NAT-2026 once built. Login is CommCare HQ OAuth (separate from the labs Connect session).
items.claim_hint: This is a real, running product a campaign administrator can use today.

items.summary: The national synthetic campaign is stood up in-app via the connect_labs MCP (campaign_build_national) — direct DB + real GeoPoDe boundaries, no AWS/ECS task.
items.claim_hint: The full national dataset can be generated on demand against real geography.

items.ref: labs prod DB — national campaign (code MR-NAT-2026) build status
items.summary: The 50k national campaign may not yet be built in labs prod (the MCP tool wasn't callable from the build session). Until built, /campaign/ shows the 64-worker demo; the map's boundaries/GPS need the domain (national) campaign to be populated.
items.claim_hint: GAP: the map + national-scale scenes need the national campaign stood up in labs before recording.

items.summary: Recording /campaign/ requires a CommCare-HQ-authed browser session (the campaign app uses CommCare OAuth, not the labs Connect session). The recorder must establish/import that session (Playwright + ACE_HQ creds, or an /ace:labs-login that primes the CCHQ session the campaign OAuth reuses).
items.claim_hint: GAP: the recorder needs the CommCare-OAuth auth path wired before it can capture the campaign UI.
